rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical halogenation/semi-pinacol rearrangement of allylic alcohols using MgBr2·6H2O or MgCl2 as halide source to form β-halocarbonyls with all-carbon quaternary centers
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of halide anion (Br- or Cl-) to molecular halogen (Br2 / Cl2), which then acts as the electrophilic halogen source; cathode evolves H2 from protons

key_intermediates.name: halonium ion / bromiranium ion on alkene

electron_flow: Halide anion is oxidized at the anode to molecular halogen (via Br2•- to Br2); the halogen forms a halonium ion with the allylic alcohol alkene, triggering a classic electrophilic semi-pinacol 1,2-migration of the aryl/alkyl group from the carbinol carbon to give a β-halocarbonyl, while protons are reduced to H2 at the cathode.

probe_reagents.name: BHT
probe_reagents.observation: Inhibited the reaction, suggesting radical species involvement (halogen radical/Br2•-)

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Inhibited the reaction, consistent with radical halogen intermediate

probe_reagents.name: non-symmetric migration substrate 1v (p-MeO-aryl vs p-CF3-aryl)
probe_reagents.observation: Selective migration of electron-rich p-MeO-aryl group gave 2v in 95% yield, identical to product from NBS-mediated reaction, supporting a cationic semi-pinacol migration

probe_reagents.name: NBS control
probe_reagents.observation: NBS as electrophilic Br source gave the same product 2v, supporting electrophilic Br+ / cationic pathway

notes: Authors propose that anodically generated Br2 (via Br2•-) initiates an electrophilic semi-pinacol rearrangement; the high diastereoselectivity (dr > 20:1 for 2i, 2j) and preferential migration of electron-rich aryl groups support a cationic (rather than radical) migration step. Radical inhibition by BHT/TEMPO is attributed to interception of the halogen radical intermediate during anodic Br- oxidation, not a C-centered radical on substrate. MgBr2·6H2O is preferred over MgCl2 due to lower oxidation potential of Br-.
